title: Add permission-scope severity tier to skill security audit
description: The skill security audit (soul_manager.audit_skill()) currently returns pass/fail based on dangerous patterns. Extend it to also classify the permission scope of passing skills into tiers (e.g., read-only, write-local, exec, network-external) and log a severity-multiplier warning for skills with broad scope — even when they pass the malware checks. This turns the audit from a binary gate into a risk-annotated log.
rationale: The reading note's core insight: '权限控制不只是攻击面问题，是 incident severity 的乘数.' The existing audit blocks clearly malicious skills, but a skill that legitimately requests broad filesystem write or unconstrained network access silently passes — with no record of the elevated blast radius if it later misbehaves or gets compromised. The 4x anchor makes this concrete: a skill with write access to all of ~/Sandbox/ isn't just a wider attack surface, it's a severity multiplier on any future incident. Logging scope tiers makes this multiplier visible and auditable.
diff_description: In audit_skill(), after the existing malware checks pass, add a second-pass scope classifier: (1) scan the skill code for filesystem patterns (open/write/os.remove/shutil) and classify as 'write-local' if present; (2) scan for network patterns (requests/urllib/curl/socket) and classify as 'network-external' if present; (3) scan for subprocess/exec patterns and classify as 'exec' if present. Assign a severity tier (LOW/MEDIUM/HIGH) based on the broadest scope found. Log this tier alongside the pass result: e.g., 'AUDIT PASS [severity=HIGH: exec+network-external] skill_name'. Optionally add a config threshold in config.py (SKILL_MAX_SEVERITY_TIER) — skills above the threshold require explicit user confirmation before being saved, matching the pattern of HARD RULE 3 for Substack publishing.
